name: Cuatro Pueblos Trail
name_original: Ruta de los 4 Pueblos – circular desde Reyero (PR-LE 49)
description: Mountain bike the moderate 13.5-mile Cuatro Pueblos Trail in Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, featuring mountain passes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Reyero offers a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, including parts of the Macizo de los Mampodres, and regional parks. The area features varied elevations, from challenging climbs to winding descents through natural environments. Riders can expect trails that traverse open hillsides, forested sections, and rural paths connecting small villages. This region provides a robust setting for mountain biking, with routes catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Reyero The most popular mountain bike trail is Susarón Loop Trail, a 15.4 miles (24.7 km) route that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate trail offers a balanced mix of ascents and descents through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta al Macizo de los Mampodres – Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre desde Puebla de Lillo, a difficult 31.5 miles (50.8 km) path. This route explores the mountainous terrain of the regional park, featuring significant elevation changes and expansive views. Local mountain bikers also love the Cuatro Pueblos Trail, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Reyero is defined by mountainous terrain, regional park landscapes, and rural paths. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels, with no easy routes available. The routes in Reyero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from around 15 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Reyero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Reyero?
A: Mountain biking around Reyero primarily offers moderate and difficult routes. There are no easy trails available, so riders should be prepared for varied elevations and challenging terrain. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes and regional parks.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Reyero?
A: The trails around Reyero are generally not suitable for beginners, as all routes are classified as either moderate or difficult. Riders should have some experience with mountain biking to safely enjoy the terrain.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Reyero?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040463767/vuelta-al-macizo-de-los-mampodres-parque-regional-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre-desde-puebla-de-lillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Macizo de los Mampodres&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 31.5-mile (50.8 km) path with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Cotado Pass – Collado de Viego loop from Crémenes&lt;/a&gt;, covering 39.2 miles (63 km) with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Reyero?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Reyero are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037751012/susaron-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 15.4-mile (24.7 km) moderate route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Cotado Pass – Church of Lois loop from Crémenes&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 21.1-mile (34 km) ride.

Q: What can I expect to see along the mountain bike trails in Reyero?
A: The trails in Reyero offer diverse scenery, including mountainous terrain, regional park landscapes, and rural paths connecting small villages. You can also find impressive peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón Peak (1,878 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yordas (1,967 m)&lt;/a&gt;, as well as mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5516418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Lois&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Cotado Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Reyero?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Reyero is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn, when the weather is more stable and trails are dry. Winter conditions can bring snow and ice, making many routes impassable or significantly more challenging.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in Reyero?
A: Trail durations vary depending on the route and your pace. Moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040417140/cuatro-pueblos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuatro Pueblos Trail&lt;/a&gt; (13.5 miles / 21.7 km) can take around 2 hours 18 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040463767/vuelta-al-macizo-de-los-mampodres-parque-regional-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre-desde-puebla-de-lillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Macizo de los Mampodres&lt;/a&gt; (31.5 miles / 50.8 km), can take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Reyero?
A: Given that all mountain bike routes in Reyero are classified as moderate or difficult, there are no trails specifically recommended as family-friendly for younger children or absolute beginners. The terrain requires a certain level of skill and endurance.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Reyero?
A: The mountain bike trails in Reyero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from around 15 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse mountainous terrain, the expansive views within the regional park, and the natural environments encountered along the routes.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Reyero?
A: There are 17 mountain bike trails available around Reyero, offering a range of moderate and difficult options for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Reyero?
A: The terrain around Reyero is characterized by mountainous landscapes, including parts of the Macizo de los Mampodres, and regional parks. You can expect varied elevations, from challenging climbs to winding descents, traversing open hillsides, forested sections, and rural paths.
tips: Or also known as Collado Grande, we will have to cross a cattle gate to cross it. Once there, we will find a variety of fauna living next to the large Pyrenean oaks that grow there. It is a transit area of the route of the 4 towns and offers us a beautiful panoramic view.

If we are doing the Route of the 4 towns, this will be one of the hardest points of the route due to the constant climb that passes through the refuge and heads to the Viego pass, located at 1412 meters of altitude.

Rolling along the path that takes us from Valbuena de Roblo to the Viego pass, we will find a bit of everything; areas of good soil and sections of slopes with very broken soil. At various points along the route there are gates to control the movement of cattle, so it is important to leave them closed as we pass.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Cuatro Pueblos Trail for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: The Cuatro Pueblos Trail is rated as moderate. It features a mix of terrain suitable for intermediate riders, with some flowy sections and elevation changes. While it offers a good challenge, it's generally accessible for those with some mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is primarily singletrack, winding through woodlands and navigating various arroyos. Riders can expect sections with good flow, but also some areas that might present rocky or technical challenges, including loose shale. The varied terrain keeps the ride engaging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cuatro Pueblos Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for the 'Cuatro Pueblos Trail' are not detailed, the route is described as 'circular desde Reyero'. It is advisable to look for parking options within or near Reyero, which likely serves as a common starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Cuatro Pueblos Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cuatro Pueblos Trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, allowing for a continuous ride without needing a shuttle or retracing your path.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes by several interesting points. You will encounter the Collado de Prado Pando and the Collado de Viego, both mountain passes offering scenic views. There is also a Shepherds' Shelter, which provides a glimpse into the local heritage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Cuatro Pueblos Trail?
faqs.answer: The region often benefits from a longer riding season compared to other parts of Colorado, making it a good option for mountain biking even into the winter months when other trails might be snow-covered. However, spring and fall generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Cuatro Pueblos Trail?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs can vary within natural parks. As this trail is located in Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, it is generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always check local signage for specific rules.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Cuatro Pueblos Trail or Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for accessing trails within Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre for activities like mountain biking. However, it's always good practice to check the official park website or local information centers for any updated regulations or specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cuatro Pueblos Trail integrates with other paths in the area. While riding, you will also pass through sections of the 'Camino Primajas-Corniero', 'camino Viego-Vabuena de Roblo', 'Calle del Turco', 'senda a Ciguera', and 'camino Primajas', allowing for varied route options.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers scenic views as it traverses through woodlands and mountain passes. You can expect panoramic vistas of the surrounding natural landscape, characteristic of the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, including distant peaks and valleys.
